differential_diagnoses: Metastatic carcinoma (e.g., lung or occult primary with ocular metastasis)
Systemic fungal infection (e.g., Blastomycosis or Coccidioidomycosis)
Septic emboli secondary to hepatic abscess
next_steps: Perform image-guided biopsy of pulmonary nodule or liver mass for culture and histopathology
Blood cultures (aerobic, anaerobic, and specifically for B. pseudomallei)
Ophthalmology consultation for diagnostic anterior chamber or vitreous paracentesis
Transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Consult infectious disease specialist for targeted antibiotic therapy (e.g., ceftazidime or meropenem if melioidosis suspected)
Repeat neuroimaging or systemic search for metastatic disease if biopsy is non-diagnostic

differential_diagnoses: Hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae (hvKP) syndrome with endogenous endophthalmitis
Metastatic malignancy with paraneoplastic or infectious complications
next_steps: Obtain HbA1c and HIV testing to evaluate for underlying immunocompromise
Perform image-guided aspiration and drainage of the hepatic mass for culture, gram stain, and histopathology
Perform diagnostic vitrectomy or vitreous tap for culture and PCR
Order specific serum testing for Burkholderia pseudomallei (IHA titers) and fungal markers (Beta-D-glucan)
Consult infectious disease, ophthalmology, and interventional radiology services immediately
Perform transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
